channel.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive
channel.description: A daily analysis of all of the AI and global news of the day I find from subscribing to a bunch of interesting newsletters
channel.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
channel.itunes:summary: A daily analysis of all of the AI and global news of the day I find from subscribing to a bunch of interesting newsletters

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 26, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: How AI models started building themselves (recursive self-improvement via MiniMax M2.7, OpenAI Codex, Google AlphaEvolve), Apple's deeper-than-known partnership with Google Gemini including distillation rights, Jeff Bezos and private equity firms boosting AI demand, judge calling Pentagon's actions against Anthropic "troubling", Meta laying off hundreds and naming Bosworth to lead AI-for-Work, Arm's new AI server CPU backed by Meta and OpenAI, software stocks plunging on AWS AI automation fears, Blackstone nearing $10B+ deal for data center firm Rowan, AMD-backed Vultr seeking $1B raise, the AI pause debate fallacy, and vibe coding safety failures at Amazon, Meta, and Replit.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: How AI models started building themselves (recursive self-improvement via MiniMax M2.7, OpenAI Codex, Google AlphaEvolve), Apple's deeper-than-known partnership with Google Gemini including distillation rights, Jeff Bezos and private equity firms boosting AI demand, judge calling Pentagon's actions against Anthropic "troubling", Meta laying off hundreds and naming Bosworth to lead AI-for-Work, Arm's new AI server CPU backed by Meta and OpenAI, software stocks plunging on AWS AI automation fears, Blackstone nearing $10B+ deal for data center firm Rowan, AMD-backed Vultr seeking $1B raise, the AI pause debate fallacy, and vibe coding safety failures at Amazon, Meta, and Replit.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 23, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI's strategic toolchain grab with Astral acquisition (uv, Ruff, ty) plus GPT-5.4 mini/nano compressing the AI startup space, SEC/CFTC joint crypto taxonomy giving builders a regulatory map, Fed holding rates amid elevated uncertainty, Nasdaq Private Market tender offers normalizing as talent strategy, X-energy's draft S-1 for nuclear IPO, DOJ indictment on AI chip smuggling to China, Sam Harris on the moral complexity of the US-Iran war (right war, wrong people), Trivium China on Beijing's energy security vindication and strategic reserves insulating China from oil shock, the postponed Xi-Trump summit, and Sinica's Chinese phrase of the week on "pitfall majors" as AI reshapes university education.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI's strategic toolchain grab with Astral acquisition (uv, Ruff, ty) plus GPT-5.4 mini/nano compressing the AI startup space, SEC/CFTC joint crypto taxonomy giving builders a regulatory map, Fed holding rates amid elevated uncertainty, Nasdaq Private Market tender offers normalizing as talent strategy, X-energy's draft S-1 for nuclear IPO, DOJ indictment on AI chip smuggling to China, Sam Harris on the moral complexity of the US-Iran war (right war, wrong people), Trivium China on Beijing's energy security vindication and strategic reserves insulating China from oil shock, the postponed Xi-Trump summit, and Sinica's Chinese phrase of the week on "pitfall majors" as AI reshapes university education.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 22, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Silicon Valley's AI enthusiasm chasm vs. the rest of the world's skepticism (Oscars AI drama, Hachette's "Shy Girl" removal, gamer backlash to Nvidia DLSS), Jensen Huang's $1T Nvidia sales forecast at GTC, AI startups splurging on Bay Area private parties (Vercel's Wolfsbane dinner), OpenAI's first advertisers unable to prove ChatGPT ads work, Anduril's $4B Ohio weapons factory "Arsenal 1", Apple's attempted Halide camera app acquisition and the co-founder fraud scandal that followed, Exponential View on Pakistan's solar shield saving $6.3B amid the Iran energy war, the shift to an inference-first economy, AI models training their own successors (MiniMax M2.7), and the importance of not delegating human judgment to AI agents.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Silicon Valley's AI enthusiasm chasm vs. the rest of the world's skepticism (Oscars AI drama, Hachette's "Shy Girl" removal, gamer backlash to Nvidia DLSS), Jensen Huang's $1T Nvidia sales forecast at GTC, AI startups splurging on Bay Area private parties (Vercel's Wolfsbane dinner), OpenAI's first advertisers unable to prove ChatGPT ads work, Anduril's $4B Ohio weapons factory "Arsenal 1", Apple's attempted Halide camera app acquisition and the co-founder fraud scandal that followed, Exponential View on Pakistan's solar shield saving $6.3B amid the Iran energy war, the shift to an inference-first economy, AI models training their own successors (MiniMax M2.7), and the importance of not delegating human judgment to AI agents.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 21, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: CIA venture arm IQT invests in Prometheus Hyperscale AI data centers in Wyoming, Nvidia Robotics chief Deepu Talla on how AI agents will bring a ChatGPT moment for robotics, OpenAI plans a desktop SuperApp combining ChatGPT, Codex and browser Atlas, 8 key investor questions about the upcoming SpaceX mega-IPO, and Sol Rashidi's essential take on preserving critical thinking in the age of AI.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: CIA venture arm IQT invests in Prometheus Hyperscale AI data centers in Wyoming, Nvidia Robotics chief on how AI agents will bring a ChatGPT moment for robotics, OpenAI plans a desktop SuperApp combining ChatGPT, Codex and browser Atlas, 8 key investor questions about the upcoming SpaceX mega-IPO, and Sol Rashidi's essential take on preserving critical thinking in the age of AI.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 20, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Microsoft grows concerned OpenAI-AWS deal may violate Azure contract, Meta shutters Horizon VR, the US-led push to break China's grip on LFP batteries for EVs and AI data centers, ex-Andreessen GP Anjney Midha launching AMP — a 'grid for AI' servers, how Cohesity is using Claude Code to replace ServiceNow and Splunk, Giga Energy's bootstrapped $270M data center startup, SEC considering halving earnings reports to unlock the IPO market, Amazon acquires Swiss-Mile robotics startup Rivr, NFX on what artists know that founders don't in the AI era, and Sinica on a stark US-China warning from Wang Jisi and Mike Lampton.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Microsoft grows concerned OpenAI-AWS deal may violate Azure contract, Meta shutters Horizon VR, the US-led push to break China's grip on LFP batteries, ex-Andreessen GP Anjney Midha launching AMP — a 'grid for AI' servers, how Cohesity is using Claude Code to replace ServiceNow and Splunk, Giga Energy's bootstrapped data center story, SEC considering halving earnings reports to unlock the IPO market, Amazon acquires Rivr robotics startup, NFX on what artists know that founders don't, and Sinica on a stark US-China warning.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 19, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Meta's rogue AI agent security incident (Sev 1 classification), Nvidia NemoClaw open-source agent framework at GTC, Apple cracking down on vibe coding apps in the App Store, Coinbase betting on AI agent payments with Cloudflare stablecoin deal, Nscale's risky $14.6B AI data center bet in West Virginia, Microsoft Copilot leadership overhaul under Satya Nadella, Amazon AWS targeting $600B in sales, and how AI is transforming marketing through clipping and hyper-segmentation.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Meta's rogue AI agent security incident, Nvidia NemoClaw at GTC, Apple cracking down on vibe coding apps, Coinbase's AI agent payments bet, Nscale's risky data center play, Microsoft Copilot overhaul, Amazon AWS targeting $600B in sales, and AI transforming marketing through clipping and hyper-segmentation.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 18, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Nvidia GTC conference highlights with Groq chip integration tackling AI inference tasks, OpenAI signs new AWS deal for US government and Pentagon contracts, Palantir's enterprise AI dominance over OpenAI and Anthropic in delivering end-to-end AI solutions, VC concerns about AI app durability at the Montgomery Summit, Alibaba's major AI reorganization under new Token Hub division, Meta's $27B deal with Nebius for AI data centers, OpenAI forming joint ventures with PE firms including TPG and Bain, and the college athlete NIL payment debate from The Free Press.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Nvidia GTC conference highlights with Groq chip integration, OpenAI signs new AWS deal for US government and Pentagon contracts, Palantir's enterprise AI dominance, VC concerns about AI app durability, Alibaba's AI reorganization, Meta's $27B Nebius deal, OpenAI forming PE joint ventures, and the college athlete NIL payment debate.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 17, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: xAI being rebuilt from scratch by Musk with key hires from Cursor and Thinking Machines Lab, 5 ingenious data center power hacks (tent cities, jet engines, orphaned green hydrogen sites), Musk's $109B OpenAI lawsuit heads to trial April 27, Travis Kalanick plotting new self-driving company with Uber backing, Meta planning 20% layoffs amid $115B AI capex, AI hallucinations reframed as 'shameless guesses' (Astral Codex Ten), why AI still hasn't beaten cancer (Gary Marcus), Andrew Yang on taxing AI bots, China's unbreakable 80% EV battery dominance, and key data including semiconductor sales up 46% YoY.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: xAI being rebuilt from scratch by Musk with key hires from Cursor and Thinking Machines Lab, 5 ingenious data center power hacks (tent cities, jet engines, orphaned green hydrogen sites), Musk's $109B OpenAI lawsuit heads to trial April 27, Travis Kalanick plotting new self-driving company with Uber backing, Meta planning 20% layoffs amid $115B AI capex, AI hallucinations reframed as 'shameless guesses' (Astral Codex Ten), why AI still hasn't beaten cancer (Gary Marcus), Andrew Yang on taxing AI bots, China's unbreakable 80% EV battery dominance, and key data including semiconductor sales up 46% YoY.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 16, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: AI agents threatening enterprise software companies like Figma, HubSpot, and Workday (and what their SEC filings reveal vs. what CEOs say publicly), ByteDance suspends Seedance 2.0 global launch after Hollywood copyright disputes, a major inflection point in AI scaling debate as Sam Altman concedes breakthroughs beyond scaling are needed (with Meta and xAI also stumbling), Exponential View on autoresearch and the solar supercycle, Sweetgreen targeting protein-obsessed techies, and Patrick Collison on tech media palace intrigue.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: AI agents threatening enterprise software (Figma, HubSpot, Workday), ByteDance Seedance 2.0 halted by Hollywood copyright disputes, Sam Altman concedes scaling limits with Meta and xAI stumbling, Exponential View on autoresearch and solar supercycle, Sweetgreen's protein-obsessed tech strategy, and Patrick Collison on tech media palace intrigue.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 15, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Sweetgreen's protein-obsessed tech strategy to win back customers, ByteDance halts Seedance 2.0 global launch over Hollywood copyright disputes, Patrick Collison on tech media palace intrigue vs. boring incumbents, Gary Marcus on expensive evidence that AI scaling isn't enough (Meta and xAI setbacks), and Exponential View on the solar supercycle, China's OpenClaw AI agent revolution, Andrej Karpathy's autoresearch breakthrough, and how interdependence beats sovereignty in AI chips.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Sweetgreen's protein-obsessed tech strategy, ByteDance halts Seedance 2.0 over Hollywood copyright disputes, Patrick Collison on tech media palace intrigue, Gary Marcus on expensive evidence that AI scaling isn't enough, and Exponential View on the solar supercycle, China's OpenClaw AI agent revolution, Karpathy's autoresearch breakthrough, and AI interdependence beats sovereignty.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 14, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Meta delays Avocado AI model launch to May, former Anthropic researchers launch Mirendil scientific AI startup targeting $1B valuation, Travis Kalanick plots new self-driving venture with Levandowski and Uber backing, SpaceX eyes Alibaba-style bank split for monster IPO, Musk's $109B OpenAI lawsuit enters critical trial phase, GPT-5.4 highlights and AI on mobile from DeepLearning.AI The Batch, Azeem Azhar on protecting your thinking while using AI constantly, Kings League's internet-first soccer disruption and US expansion, China's 15th Five Year Plan and compute ambitions, and Oussama Ammar on the journalism crisis.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Meta delays Avocado AI model to May, Mirendil scientific AI startup from ex-Anthropic researchers targets $1B valuation, Travis Kalanick plus Levandowski plus Uber self-driving venture, SpaceX monster IPO bank strategy, Musk vs OpenAI $109B lawsuit trial, GPT-5.4 and AI on mobile, Azeem Azhar on cognitive offloading vs surrender, Kings League sports media disruption, China 15th Five Year Plan, and journalism crisis.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 13, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic in talks with Blackstone and PE firms to form AI consulting venture, Nscale/Nvidia eyeing major West Virginia data center site ahead of IPO, Google-Wiz deal completion greenlights tech M&A surge, Oracle offering free AI features to combat SaaS apocalypse, xAI hires two senior Cursor leaders as Musk admits coding gap, enterprises racing to make OpenClaw safe, Winklevoss crypto losses post-IPO, Trump picks conventional electric aircraft over flying taxis, Gary Marcus on Pentagon's illogical Anthropic supply chain risk ruling, Ethan Mollick on the shift to AI agent management era, and Azeem Azhar on the solar supercycle replacing fossil fuels.

item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic in talks with Blackstone and PE firms to form AI consulting venture, Nscale/Nvidia eyeing major West Virginia data center site, Google-Wiz deal greenlights M&A surge, Oracle offering free AI features, xAI hires from Cursor, enterprises racing to make OpenClaw safe, Winklevoss crypto losses, Trump picks conventional electric aircraft, Pentagon's illogical Anthropic supply chain risk ruling, Ethan Mollick on the AI agent era, and Azeem Azhar on the solar supercycle.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 12, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic in talks with Blackstone and PE firms to form AI consulting venture, Microsoft in talks to lease large Texas data center site after Oracle walked away, Iran war imperiling AI funding and data center expansion, CXL computing standard challenges, plus Substack essays on growth and loneliness.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 11, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI plans to integrate Sora video AI into ChatGPT in strategy shift, Tencent's secret WeChat AI agent project targeting 1.4B users, Microsoft's new AI Office bundle, AI startups developing scientific discovery tools, the debate about public sentiment toward AI, Gary Marcus on AI coding outages and science fraud, and more from The Information, The AI Agenda, and Marcus on AI.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 10, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Spark Capital targeting $3 billion in new funds after nearly 100x Anthropic gains, OpenAI walking away from Oracle Stargate expansion in Abilene, Anthropic likely to win its promised lawsuit against the DoD, OpenAI's IPO hopes facing skeptical investors, BYD fighting back with a new battery amid sliding sales, OpenAI's robotics head quitting over Defense Dept deal, Claude commanding nearly 70% of US business AI subscriptions, and Ozempic linked to 50% fewer drug-related deaths.

item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Spark Capital targeting $3B in new funds after Anthropic gains, OpenAI walking away from Oracle Stargate in Abilene, Anthropic vs DoD lawsuit, OpenAI IPO skepticism, BYD's new battery, OpenAI robotics head quits, Claude's 70% market share, and Ozempic's new health benefits.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 9, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Iran war imperiling $300 billion in Gulf AI spending affecting data centers by Amazon, OpenAI, xAI and others (The Information), OpenAI's massive $110B raise at $730B valuation and venture capital mega-rounds (Ignite Insights), The World the Fatwa Made examining geopolitical impacts (The Free Press), China economic and trade analysis including GDP and NEVs (Trivium China/Sinica), and startup investment strategies (Hall T Martin).

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 8, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Iranian drone strikes on AWS data centers in Bahrain and UAE, AI compute geopolitics and US chip export controls, Acemoglu's paper on AI shrinking the knowledge commons, US productivity growth hitting 2.8% in the agent era, Sam Altman controversy and OpenAI boycott movement, GPT-5.4 release, Paul Graham on Brand Age, and Jungian psychology perspectives.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 7, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Anthropic-Pentagon feud deepens as Dario Amodei's leaked internal memo attacking OpenAI derails talks, Anne Wojcicki's $305M nonprofit plan to revive 23andMe with rich donors and MAHA connections, David Ellison's transformation of Paramount into a Gen Z-focused media empire, plus Substack perspectives on geopolitical shifts and labor politics.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 6, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Quince retail startup eyeing $10B+ valuation with $2B revenue run rate, Anthropic spoiling the software debate at Morgan Stanley's tech conference, Nvidia cloud ally Together AI raising at $7.5B valuation, Alibaba moving faster than Amazon and OpenAI in AI for commerce, Anduril forecasting $4B+ in sales with $1B in losses, OpenAI developing bidirectional audio model for voice assistants, China's homegrown memory suppliers boosted by Alibaba demand, Iran conflict setback for flying taxi companies, AI context layers as the newest category, Niall Ferguson on potential WWIII, Gary Marcus on AI reliability for taxes, and Auren Hoffman on AI-driven baby boom.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Quince eyeing $10B+ valuation, Anthropic at Morgan Stanley tech conference, Together AI raising at $7.5B, Alibaba's AI commerce push, Anduril's $4B sales forecast, OpenAI's bidirectional audio model, China's memory chip suppliers, flying taxi setback from Iran conflict, AI context layers, Niall Ferguson on WWIII, Gary Marcus on AI reliability, and AI-driven baby boom prediction.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 5, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI tops $25 billion in annualized revenue while Anthropic narrows the gap to $19 billion, OpenAI selects Cooley and Wachtell law firms for potential blockbuster IPO at $730B valuation, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei's 1,600-word memo attacking OpenAI's Pentagon deal as 'safety theater', OpenAI holds early talks with The Trade Desk to sell ads in ChatGPT, OpenAI scales back shopping plans for ChatGPT, SpaceX pitches Starlink to wary telecom firms, OpenAI's next AI model with 'extreme' reasoning, OpenClaw disrupting China's tech landscape, and OpenAI developing an internal alternative to GitHub.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI tops $25 billion in annualized revenue while Anthropic narrows the gap to $19 billion, OpenAI selects Cooley and Wachtell law firms for potential blockbuster IPO at $730B valuation, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei's 1,600-word memo attacking OpenAI's Pentagon deal as 'safety theater', OpenAI holds early talks with The Trade Desk to sell ads in ChatGPT, OpenAI scales back shopping plans for ChatGPT, SpaceX pitches Starlink to wary telecom firms, OpenAI's next AI model with 'extreme' reasoning, OpenClaw disrupting China's tech landscape, and OpenAI developing an internal alternative to GitHub.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 4, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Lux Capital's stark warning about AI startup bubble with "less than 10 that matter," Anthropic-Palantir partnership at risk after Pentagon ruling, OpenAI developing internal GitHub alternative, Amazon exploring chatbot ad sales for other apps, OpenAI's updated Pentagon agreement with stronger surveillance protections, the "Civilization Trap" in geopolitics as the post-American order emerges, Princeton study on sycophantic AI distorting beliefs, harness engineering as the new discipline for supervising AI-generated code, and a personal essay on choosing hope over AI doom.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Lux Capital's stark warning about AI startup bubble, Anthropic-Palantir partnership at risk, OpenAI developing GitHub alternative, Amazon exploring chatbot ads, OpenAI Pentagon agreement updates, the Civilization Trap in geopolitics, sycophantic AI distorting beliefs, harness engineering for AI-generated code, and choosing hope over AI doom.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 3, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Pentagon declares Anthropic a "supply chain risk" but prediction markets shrug it off, 2026 midterm election forecasting and potential voting law changes, Iran conflict analysis after Khamenei's death, Groundhog Day accuracy analysis, new crypto prediction market MNX from Manifold founders, plus Weekly AI Roundup with Google Nano Banana 2, Taalas AI chips, Perplexity Computer, Anthropic Claude Code features, and more.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - March 2, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: US-Israel strikes kill Khamenei launching Operation Epic Fury and Iranian retaliation, deep analysis of OpenAI's "all lawful use" military contract and its surveillance/autonomous weapons loopholes vs Anthropic's refusal, Gary Marcus on AI potentially killing civilians in Iran strikes, Thrive and Andreessen Horowitz leading fastest-growing VC firms with $34B raised while zombie VCs multiply, ByteDance's Seedance 2.0 creating disturbingly realistic deepfakes from single photos, and Democrats' need for structural change in Black voter engagement.

item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: Google's multibillion-dollar TPU chip deal with Meta challenging Nvidia, OpenAI's M&A war chest and $100B fundraising, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei's historic statement refusing Pentagon demands for unrestricted AI weapons and mass surveillance, Meta's internal chip design roadblocks, Amazon's $50B OpenAI investment tied to IPO/AGI, Form Energy's iron-air battery deal with nuclear plants, enterprise AI tensions between incumbents and startups, and what comes after SaaS.

item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI's scramble for computing power after the $500B Stargate project stalled, AI consciousness fellowships and alignment seminars, the SAVE Act's hidden voter suppression threats, ECPM as the future-ready skills curriculum replacing STEM, and philosophical perspectives on pain and transcendence from Schopenhauer to Pessoa.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI's scramble for computing power after the $500B Stargate project stalled, AI consciousness fellowships and alignment seminars, the SAVE Act's hidden voter suppression threats, ECPM as the future-ready skills curriculum replacing STEM, and philosophical perspectives on pain and transcendence from Schopenhauer to Pessoa.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - February 22, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: AI agents and the tedium frontier, the productivity staircase showing AI finally appearing in the statistics, the economics of pay-to-execute agent costs and open-source alternatives, robot insurance and minimum wage effects on automation, Pentagon using Claude via Palantir, pigeon drones with neural implants, and more from Exponential View.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: AI agents and the tedium frontier, the productivity staircase showing AI finally appearing in the statistics, the economics of pay-to-execute agent costs and open-source alternatives, robot insurance and minimum wage effects on automation, Pentagon using Claude via Palantir, pigeon drones with neural implants, and more from Exponential View.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - February 21, 2026 (Weekend Edition)
item.description: Weekend roundup covering: Google's aggressive move into AI music generation threatening Spotify and startups, the booming high-tech home sauna trend in Silicon Valley, and a fascinating deep dive into longevity startup Mitrix Bio's radical mitochondrial transplant approach — including the story of 91-year-old physicist John Cramer's quest to extend his life.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Weekend roundup covering: Google's aggressive move into AI music generation threatening Spotify and startups, the booming high-tech home sauna trend in Silicon Valley, and a fascinating deep dive into longevity startup Mitrix Bio's radical mitochondrial transplant approach — including the story of 91-year-old physicist John Cramer's quest to extend his life.

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - February 21, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and global news roundup covering: OpenAI's boosted revenue forecasts and $112B cash burn through 2030, OpenAI's 200-person AI device team building smart speakers, Silicon Valley's longevity race, AMD backstops $300M Crusoe loan, Supreme Court strikes down Trump's tariffs, GLM-5 as new open-weights AI leader, Big AI's $100M+ lobbying spend, Azeem Azhar's personal AI agent experience, the trillion-agent economy, Texas aerospace ecosystem, Meta cutting stock awards, AI predicting illness from sleep data, and women mourning deleted AI boyfriends.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione

item.title: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive - February 20, 2026
item.description: Today's AI and tech news roundup covering: eBay acquires Depop for $1.2B, Amazon's AI employee tracking via Clarity, AMD backstops $300M Crusoe AI chip loan, ZaiNar's $1B GPS alternative for physical AI, Hungryroot eyes 2026 IPO after 55% growth, Ford's $30K EV pickup bet, Zuckerberg testifies in social media trial, Perplexity drops ads, and more from The Information.
item.itunes:author: Lorenzo Thione
item.itunes:summary: Today's AI and tech news roundup covering: eBay acquires Depop for $1.2B, Amazon's AI employee tracking via Clarity, AMD backstops $300M Crusoe AI chip loan, ZaiNar's $1B GPS alternative for physical AI, Hungryroot eyes 2026 IPO after 55% growth, Ford's $30K EV pickup bet, Zuckerberg testifies in social media trial, Perplexity drops ads, and more from The Information.

item.title: Welcome to Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive
item.description: Lorenzo's Daily Deep Dive is a daily AI-generated podcast covering the latest in AI, tech, and business news curated from top newsletters. New episodes every morning.
